prompt: I'm pitching a new dashboard feature to stakeholders tomorrow. I don't need a working prototype — just a quick wireframe showing the layout with placeholder data. It's a metrics overview with 3 KPI cards and a time-series chart.
We're debating two different navigation structures for our app. Can you sketch both as rough ASCII layouts so we have something concrete to compare instead of talking in circles?

I need a throwaway HTML page that shows what our pricing page could look like with a three-tier layout. Use lorem ipsum and fake prices. The point is to get alignment on the structure before design starts.
We're considering adding a command palette to our web app. Before I write a design doc, can you mock up a quick sketch of how it would look and behave? Just enough to communicate the idea.

The product team wants to see what an email notification for 'build failed' would look like. Can you whip up a rough text-based mockup of the email body with placeholder variables? Don't overthink it — this is just to start the conversation.

I want to propose a new CLI interface for our internal tool. Can you sketch out what the help output would look like for the main command and two subcommands? This is purely to communicate the UX concept.

Before I build a real database schema, I want to sketch out the entity-relationship diagram for a simple blog platform: users, posts, comments, tags. Just the rough shape — no need for exact column types.
Our team is debating whether the settings page should be a sidebar or a tabbed layout. Mock up both patterns with placeholder settings groups so we can react to something real instead of opinions.

I need to show my manager what a Slack notification from our new alerting system would look like. Create a quick mockup of the Slack message with blocks, including a severity indicator, summary, and action buttons.

We're exploring whether to add a dark mode to our app. Before anyone writes CSS, can you create a rough visual comparison — just describe or sketch what the key screens would look like in dark vs light?
